concerns.text: FR-002 mandates quantization at bit depths 1, 8, 10, 12, 14, and 16. Tasks T014 and T021 explicitly restrict the pilot implementation to only 1, 8, 12, and 16, omitting 10-bit and 14-bit. This is a silent weakening of the functional requirement without an explicit 'staged' exception in the spec.

SC-005 requires measuring reproducibility across 10 independent runs of '[deferred] signals' (implying the full statistically representative sample). Task T031 limits this to 10 runs of the N=800 pilot batch. This reduces the statistical power and signal diversity required by the success criterion, violating the measurement definition.

US-1 Acceptance Scenario 1 requires verifying that '[deferred] of the resulting strain data' has SNR within [8, 50]. Task T013a enforces a strict '±0.5 tolerance' on the distribution coverage. The spec allows for a stratified sampling strategy (per research.md) which bins SNR, not a continuous uniform distribution. The task's strict tolerance constraint is not supported by the spec's acceptance criteria and may force an invalid generation strategy.
